purpose: Block B5 — single source of truth for cutover-gating item status. The CI test at tests/invariants/cutover-gate.test.ts reads this manifest and asserts every required item is verified=wired-and-exercised before runtime/cutover-flag.ts:CUTOVER_ENABLED can be set to true. Schema v2 adds the `verification` field after audit revealed many items were marked done but not actually exercised in production paths. `status` records whether the work was performed; `verification` records whether the work is doing its job in production. Block E readiness requires status=done AND verification=wired-and-exercised. Phase→Block lineage (the original routing refactor's Phases 0-7 are subsumed): Phase 0 → docs/ROUTING_STATE_MACHINE.md. Phase 1 → routing types + registry + codemod (DONE). Phase 2 → routing dispatcher + matrix tests (DONE, B5). Phase 3 → routing shadow mode (DONE). Phase 5 → v2 spec-edit wart fixes (DONE, FLAG-1..5). Phase 4 → Block E (cutover). Phase 6 → Block F1 (cleanup). Phase 7 → Block F2/F3 (Coder/Reviewer agents).

verification_definitions.burn-in: Work shipped; in legitimate burn-in window collecting real-traffic evidence.
verification_definitions.docs-only: Documentation written. Never exercised against real conditions (no drill, no real onboarding, no real incident).
verification_definitions.infrastructure-only: Code/module/script exists with tests, but is NOT WIRED into production callers, OR is narrower in scope than its title claims. Capability exists but is not yet doing its job.
verification_definitions.wired-and-exercised: Code is in production callers AND tested AND verified to fire by synthetic-violation testing. The capability is actively doing its job.

items.verification_note: Shipped: deterministic count helpers `countPlatformGapItems` and `countAgentGapItems` in `runtime/upstream-notice-format.ts` (Principle 11, no LLM in the gate path) + post-run consolidation gate in the architect path at `interfaces/slack/handlers/message.ts`. When the architect calls `offer_upstream_revision(target=pm|design)` and the agent's question enumerates fewer items than `auditPmSpec`/`auditDesignSpec` detected, the gate overrides `pendingEscalation.question` with the consolidated platform brief from `parsePmGapText` / `parseDesignGapText`. Both PM and design targets covered per Principle 15 cross-agent parity. Log marker `[ESCALATION-GATE] B6:`. Tests: 8 unit tests for the count helpers in `tests/invariants/upstream-notice-contract.test.ts` + 5 regression tests (bug #13) in `tests/regression/architect-escalation-consolidation.test.ts` + 2 integration scenarios in `tests/integration/workflows.test.ts` (Scenario B6) covering positive (override fires when agent enumerates 1 of 3) and negative (faithful enumeration retained when agent matches platform count) cases. The integration scenario drives `handleFeatureChannelMessage` end-to-end through the architect tool_use path — `verification=wired-and-exercised` (exercised by integration tests, not just infrastructure-only). MT-20 is the optional spot-check during integration walks.

items.title: PM/Designer briefs must declare readOnly to the agent (Bug C fix)
items.verification_note: Shipped: shared constant runtime/readonly-brief-clause.ts (READONLY_AGENT_BRIEF_CLAUSE) declares the no-spec-writing-tools contract — names the canonical action-claim phrasings the agent must avoid ('Applying the patch...', 'I'll update the spec...') and names the resolution (human says yes, platform applies). Constant injected via template interpolation at all 4 readOnly brief sites in interfaces/slack/handlers/message.ts: designer→PM (line 590), designer→architect (line 605), architect→designer (line 943), architect→PM (line 959). Per Principle 15 cross-agent parity. Tests: structural invariant tests/invariants/readonly-brief-clause.test.ts (9 tests) pins both halves of the contract — the constant exists with the right semantics (4 module-level tests) AND every brief site interpolates it (5 cross-agent tests including a heading-proximity defense-in-depth check that catches new readOnly call sites lacking a brief) + 5 regression tests (bug #15) in tests/regression/readonly-brief-clause.test.ts. Verification stays infrastructure-only until MT-22 confirms agents honor the clause in real Slack — the fix is prompt-rule (probabilistic per Principle 8); structural invariant ensures the clause is always PRESENT, but not always OBEYED by the LLM. If real-Slack non-compliance recurs, v2 will add a runtime stripper (same architectural pattern as Block N2's tool-name sentence-drop) targeting action-claim verb phrases in readOnly responses.

items.title: Engineering-spec writeback must respect SSOT (Bug D fix)
items.verification_note: Shipped: codified CLAUDE.md Principle 16 (Spec write ownership — resolved decisions land only in the owner's spec; carve-outs for preseed-open-items / handoff sections explicitly documented) plus per-agent owner table. Removed patchEngineeringSpecWithDecision call from architect's upstream-revision-reply branch in interfaces/slack/handlers/message.ts (the violation: PM/designer-authored content was being recorded under `### Architect Decision (pre-engineering)` heading in the architect-owned engineering spec, append-only layout producing N duplicate-heading blocks per N escalations). Single legitimate call remains (Path A: designer→architect escalation reply, architect-authored content → engineering spec). Architect re-reads upstream spec on every run via loadArchitectAgentContext, so no information is lost. Cross-agent audit confirmed exactly one violation existed system-wide; all other writebacks (saveDraft*, saveApproved*, patch*Spec*, preseed*, seedHandoffSection, updateApprovedSpecOnMain) are owner-consistent or fall in documented carve-outs. Tests: structural invariant tests/invariants/spec-write-ownership.test.ts (19 tests — AST-greps every callsite and pins to documented allow-list; adding a new writeback requires updating manifest in same commit) + 4 regression tests in tests/regression/spec-write-ownership.test.ts (bug #14) + flipped existing integration Scenario N44b (was pinning the buggy behavior; now asserts engineering spec NOT touched and product spec IS written). MT-21 spot-check tier (integration coverage end-to-end). AGENTS.md gained per-agent owner table under 'Spec write ownership' subsection.

items.title: patchProductSpecWithRecommendations must apply category rules deterministically (Bug E fix) — both PM and design escalation paths (Principle 15 cross-agent parity)
items.verification_note: Shipped: deterministic runtime/category-rule-extractor.ts (pure, no LLM, @deterministic per Principle 11) exports extractCategoryRules / applyCategoryRules / findResidualCategoryViolations. Parses universal-quantified substitution patterns — any/all/every 'X' becomes/should be/→/-> 'Y'; replace [all] 'X' with/by 'Y'; change every 'X' to 'Y'. Conservative on quantifier — single-criterion edits without universal cue are NOT treated as category rules. Word-boundary case-insensitive substitution. Wired into BOTH escalation-spec writers (Principle 15 cross-agent parity): runtime/pm-escalation-spec-writer.ts (PM recommendations → product spec) AND runtime/design-escalation-spec-writer.ts (Designer recommendations → design spec). Same shared module — single source of truth for the parser. Pre-Haiku application + post-Haiku residual safety net (re-applies if Haiku's merge re-introduced any from-word). Log markers `[ESCALATION] B9: extracted N category rule(s)` and `[ESCALATION] B9: Haiku's merge re-introduced N category-rule term(s)`. Tests: 25 unit tests + 9 regression tests (bug #16, including 3 cross-agent-parity assertions verifying both writers are wired) + 2 integration scenarios — Scenario B9 (architect→PM via designer→PM escalation, deliberately-buggy mocked Haiku patch replaces 2 of 4 instances) and Scenario B9b (architect→designer escalation, same shape, replaces 1 of 3 instances). Both scenarios assert the saved spec has 0 surviving from-words, proving the residual safety net is the structural guarantee on both paths. MT-23 spot-check tier covers both paths. CODE_MARKER bump for the cross-agent ship: b9b-design-category-rule-cross-agent-parity. verification=wired-and-exercised.

items.title: Platform-built messages must not impersonate the agent in third person (Bug F fix)
items.verification_note: Shipped: shared constant runtime/platform-message-prefix.ts (PLATFORM_MESSAGE_PREFIX = '*Platform —*') used as the prefix for every platform-composed Slack notification. Cross-agent audit confirmed exactly 6 agent-name static prefix sites in interfaces/slack/handlers/message.ts (all in the same file, no other handlers); all 6 converted to use the shared constant + bodies rewritten in platform first-person voice ('we'll bring the PM agent back into this thread' instead of bare 'Say *yes* to bring the PM back' imperative). Structural invariant tests/invariants/platform-message-prefix.test.ts (10 tests) AST-greps every handler file's `text:` template literals and fails on any agent-name static prefix from FORBIDDEN_AGENT_PREFIXES; only ${mention} (Slack <@U…> ping or text-fallback role label addressing the human role-holder) is allowed. Tests: 5 regression tests (bug #17) in tests/regression/platform-message-prefix.test.ts including a structural assertion that no agent-name static prefix survives + integration scenario B10 in tests/integration/workflows.test.ts that drives handleFeatureChannelMessage through a re-escalation flow and asserts the actual posted message starts with '*Platform —*' and no posted message uses an agent-name prefix. Manifest verification=wired-and-exercised. MT-24 spot-check tier (3 layers of automated coverage end-to-end).

items.title: Spec-content verifier — no agent recommendation passes without verifying cited content exists (architectural gap; covers Bug G)
items.verification_note: v1 shipped: runtime/spec-content-verifier.ts (pure deterministic, @deterministic per Principle 11) exports extractAcMap, findAcReferences, extractQuotedPhrasesNear, verifyAcReferences, formatHallucinations. Detects two classes: ac-does-not-exist (cited AC# beyond spec count) and claimed-wording-not-in-ac (quoted phrase not in cited AC body). Wired log-only at PM escalation-resume site (interfaces/slack/handlers/message.ts, both arch-upstream-escalation-confirmed and arch-upstream-continuation branches) — emits [CONTENT-VERIFIER] log line on detection. 28 unit tests + 5 regression tests (bug #12, tests/regression/spec-content-hallucination-detection.test.ts) cover happy path, both hallucination classes, determinism invariant, and structural wiring assertion. v2 work (re-prompt loop + downstream patcher gating + cross-agent extension to designer + architect + brand-token / screen-name / section-heading verifiers) tracked separately. MT-19 is the real-Slack scenario; verification stays infrastructure-only until MT-19 passes.

items.title: PM hallucinates AC numbers / fabricates spec content (Bug G fix — subsumed by B11)
items.verification_note: Subsumed by B11 v1 — the AC-citation hallucination class (both ac-does-not-exist and claimed-wording-not-in-ac) is now detected at the PM escalation-resume site via runtime/spec-content-verifier.ts. v1 is log-only — operator sees [CONTENT-VERIFIER] in logs and intervenes before the spec is corrupted. v2 (re-prompt loop + patcher gating) will fully retire the user-as-line-of-defense pattern. Regression catalog bug #12. MT-19 is the real-Slack scenario.

items.title: Architect prose response disconnected from tool calls + ignored user's literal ask in favor of auto-triggered PM escalation — Principle 7 violation surfaced 2026-05-01
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-05-01 in MT walk. User asked architect to clean B8 residue from engineering spec ('Patch the engineering spec to remove the entire ## Pre-Engineering Architectural Decisions section ... show me the cleaned spec'). Architect made apply_engineering_spec_patch tool calls (invisible to user) attempting cleanup, but its prose response completely ignored the user's ask — instead surfaced an AC 27 PM-finding and said 'say yes to bring in the PM agent.' The user sees no acknowledgment of their cleanup ask, no report on what the tool calls did, and a CTA for an unrelated escalation. Likely root cause: the auto-trigger PM-escalation override at message.ts:3198+ fires whenever PM gaps exist + agent didn't call offer_upstream_revision(pm), and OVERRIDES the architect's substantive response prose with a PM-escalation CTA — even when the user's ask was completely unrelated to PM escalation. Two distinct failures: (1) prose-vs-tools mismatch (architect made tool calls but didn't report them in prose); (2) user's literal ask was silently dropped in favor of platform-driven topic switch. Both Principle 7 violations (zero human errors of omission applies symmetrically to platform too — platform must not silently change the topic on the user). Fix shape (preliminary): the auto-trigger override should be additive to the agent's response, not replacement. If the agent did substantive work in response to a user ask, the prose must report on that work; the platform may APPEND a 'P.S. — also, PM gaps detected, say yes to address them' but cannot REPLACE the agent's primary response. Cross-agent applies. Per Principle 17 also: the prose claim must agree with what the tool calls actually did.

items.title: apply_engineering_spec_patch / apply_design_spec_patch / apply_product_spec_patch lack section-remove semantics — cannot delete a section, only merge
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-05-01 in MT walk. User asked architect to remove the '## Pre-Engineering Architectural Decisions' section. Architect attempted via apply_engineering_spec_patch with a patch starting at '## Open Questions' (omitting the unwanted section). But applySpecPatch does section-merge: it replaces matching sections in the patch and KEEPS sections not in the patch — the unwanted section survived. This means cleanup of any unwanted section (B8 residue, deprecated sections, mistaken additions) is structurally impossible via the existing tool surface. Fix shape: extend applySpecPatch with a section-remove directive. Options: (a) special heading marker like '## REMOVE: ## Pre-Engineering Architectural Decisions' that removes the named section; (b) new tool apply_*_spec_patch_with_removals that takes (patch, sectionsToRemove[]) tuple; (c) full-file replacement option with explicit consent (riskier — loses partial-update safety). Affects all 3 spec writers (PM, Design, Engineering) per Principle 15 cross-agent parity. Until B15 ships, the only way to clean unwanted sections is human-side git push to the spec branch (acceptable but not platform-driven).

items.title: auditEngineeringSpec must detect B8 historical residue (stale '### Architect Decision (pre-engineering)' blocks) — Principle 7 gap surfaced 2026-05-01
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-05-01 in MT walk after B13. The engineering spec on spec/onboarding-engineering branch has 4 stale '### Architect Decision (pre-engineering)' blocks at the top from before B8 retired the writeback. The architect's state-query fast-path didn't surface this — auditEngineeringSpec doesn't recognize the residue pattern as a finding, so the architect didn't proactively flag it. User had to notice and ask for cleanup explicitly. Violates Principle 7 (zero human errors of omission — the agent must surface what the user doesn't know to ask). Fix shape: auditEngineeringSpec gains a deterministic check for the residue pattern (any '## Pre-Engineering Architectural Decisions' section OR any '### Architect Decision (pre-engineering)' subsection in the engineering spec is B8 residue and should be flagged as a finding with recommendation 'remove this section — these decisions are recorded in their proper home in the product spec on main'). Architect's auto-trigger then surfaces this finding on every state-query / readiness-summary, so the user sees 'engineering spec has B8 residue from pre-fix state' instead of having to notice the clutter manually. Symmetric for any cross-spec residue pattern — generalize the auditor to flag any duplicate-heading append pattern (auditSpecStructure already detects duplicates; cross-reference). Per Principle 17 also: this finding must flow into the readiness aggregator so all surfaces consistently report 'engineering spec has 1 residue finding' (same number across state-query, finalize-gate, escalation notifications).

items.title: Readiness aggregator must include upstream findings — state-query fast-path lies about 'Nothing blocking' when upstream specs have findings (canonical Principle 17 violation)
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-05-01 in MT walk Step 2 — became the surfacing instance for codifying CLAUDE.md Principle 17 (cross-surface message consistency). User typed 'hi' in #feature-onboarding (engineering-in-progress phase). Architect's state-query fast-path responded with `Nothing blocking — you can review and approve when ready` while in fact PM spec on main has 1 deterministic finding (vague timing 'immediately') and design spec on main has 30 findings (PLACEHOLDER markers, vague language, animation timing, form-factor coverage). The V2 readiness aggregator showed `aggregate=ready-pending-approval total=0` — `total=0` is technically true (engineering spec has no own findings) but ignores upstream chain. A reasonable user reading 'Nothing blocking' concludes the chain is clean and replies 'approved' — finalize gate then blocks them with 31 findings, contradicting the prior message. Two platform messages, same turn, factually inconsistent. Same class as B10 (platform misrepresents state) but at the readiness-aggregator surface instead of the post-action notification surface. Worse than B10 because the misleading claim drives user action. Affects BOTH legacy fast-path (interfaces/slack/handlers/message.ts) AND V2 runner (runtime/agents/runArchitectAgentV2.ts + readiness-builder.ts) since V2 shadow showed identical aggregate. Fix shape: readiness-builder.ts aggregator must call auditPmSpec(pmSpecOnMain) + auditDesignSpec(designSpecOnMain) when running in architect mode (or the analogous upstream check for designer); upstream findings must contribute to `total` and `aggregate`; rendered summary must say something like 'engineering spec drafted, but N upstream finding(s) must be resolved before approval' instead of 'Nothing blocking, approve when ready'. Symmetric for designer state-query fast-path reading PM spec. Per Principle 17: every state-query / readiness-summary / finalize-gate computation must derive from the SAME canonical SSOT (buildReadinessReport composing the deterministic auditors). Cross-surface consistency invariant tests/invariants/cross-surface-consistency.test.ts will gain a B13-specific assertion when this fix ships: when upstream findings exist, aggregate is NOT 'ready-pending-approval' and the `total` includes upstream counts.

Escalation notifications must survive bot restart within TTL (D5 fix, was Bug A)
items.verification_note: Fixed 2026-04-30 in commit (this work). EscalationNotification type now carries `timestamp` (set by setEscalationNotification). Startup uses clearStaleEntries(escalationNotifications, 'escalationNotification') with PENDING_STATE_TTL_MS (24h). 4 unit tests in tests/regression/escalation-notification-survives-restart.test.ts verify the type round-trip, timestamp stamping, and structural absence of the legacy clear-all pattern. Verification grade is `infrastructure-only` (not `wired-and-exercised`) until MT-18 runs in real Slack against a real restart — promotes to wired-and-exercised after that. Regression catalog bug #11. Resolves J3↔D5 collision.

items.title: Log-coverage gate (every state mutation has structured log line)
items.verification_note: Invariant scans only runtime/conversation-store.ts mutators. Misses message.ts post-run mutations (escalation override replacement, hedge-gate skip path), tool-handler state mutations, github-client state writes. The G1 spirit demands platform-wide; the implementation is one file.

items.title: Request-id tracing across multi-step turn
Metrics emission (counters + histograms via thin interface)
Alert hooks (cutover divergence, smoke failure, perf budget breach)
Log retention + redaction (PII/token scrubbing, per-tenant tagging)
items.verification_note: redactToken/redactPii/redactAll module + 12 unit tests, but ZERO callers in production code. The bot's logger does not redact. Real GitHub responses still get logged in plaintext.

items.title: Canonical eval dataset (50+ per agent, 30+ per classifier)
Eval runner (replay + score)
Prompt-drift regression CI test
Model-upgrade evals (run on Claude version bump)
A/B prompt comparison harness
Eval-suite criteria deterministic-tightening (drive variance below the 85% threshold)
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-05-01 during M0 reconciliation push attempt. Pre-push eval gate (npm run eval, threshold 85%) flakes between runs — observed range 83-100% across 6 consecutive runs against identical code. Root cause: most criteria are LLM-judged (Haiku as judge) on subjective predicates (substantive / specific / opinionated / discusses-X), so judge variance dominates the verdict. Variance band ~10pp puts the gate inside the noise floor. Two compounding fixes already applied (commit 70a5786) addressed the worst single-criterion offenders by replacing 2 LLM-judged criteria with deterministic structural checks per Principle 8a (architect 'it depends' detection; design empty-state structural reference) and improved the buildProductLevelPrompt 'substantive over terse' rule. Remaining work for H6: (1) audit every LLM-judged criterion across all eval scenarios (~20-25 criteria); (2) for each, decide whether it can be replaced with a deterministic check (presence of required output shape per Principle 8a) or must remain LLM-judged because the assertion is genuinely subjective; (3) for residual LLM-judged criteria, run each scenario N=5 times and compute per-criterion flake rate, drop or rewrite criteria with >20% flake; (4) restore 85% threshold after variance is provably below ±5pp. Until H6 ships, eval-gate flakes are a known noise source and operators may use --no-verify with explicit backlog reference. Does not block M0: M0 quality bar is the 24 MT catalog + cross-surface consistency + 3 crisp specs (Step 6/7), not the eval suite itself; the suite is a regression net, not the primary gate. Does not conflict with H1-H5 (those add coverage and prompt-drift detection; H6 reduces noise in existing coverage).

items.title: ADR directory + Definition-of-Done CI hook for SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md
Operator runbook (operations, on-call, common incidents)
Customer onboarding documentation (WorkspaceConfig + prerequisites)
Contribution guide for adding agents
PM/Designer escalation-confirmed brief includes structural closure clause (orchestration Gap A)
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-04-30 in BACKLOG.md lines 182-196 (orchestration continuity gaps), formalized as Block O 2026-05-01 per the canonical reconciliation plan. Today PM/Designer in escalation-confirmed mode produce only the recommendations text and stop — no closure statement (no 'Done — spec updated, escalation resolved, returning control to architect'). User has no signal that the round is complete and that control returns to the originating agent. Fix shape: shared constant in runtime/escalation-closure-clause.ts (similar pattern to runtime/readonly-brief-clause.ts from B7), injected into the closure of every PM/Designer escalation-confirmed response. Cross-agent invariant test pins presence at every escalation-resolution site. M0-required because orchestration continuity is the user's stated priority for M0.

items.title: Auto-trigger architect resume after PM/Designer applies patches (orchestration Gap B)
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-04-30 in BACKLOG.md lines 182-196, formalized as Block O 2026-05-01. Today the arch-upstream-revision-reply branch only fires when the user sends an affirmative 'yes' — no 'PM finished applying → invoke architect' continuation in the orchestrator. User must send another message to trigger architect re-run. Fix shape: V2 dispatcher (runtime/agents/dispatch.ts or equivalent) triggers architect's next turn programmatically when patchProductSpecWithRecommendations / patchDesignSpecWithRecommendations completes successfully. Net new platform behavior. Cross-agent: applies symmetrically to designer's auto-resume after architect resolves a design escalation (Gap E). M0-required.

items.title: Deterministic verifier between 'PM applied patch' and 'architect resumes' (orchestration Gap C)
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-04-30 in BACKLOG.md lines 182-196, formalized as Block O 2026-05-01. Today re-audit (auditPmSpec / auditDesignSpec) is the only gate after a writeback; doesn't structurally verify the patch wrote what was intended. If the PM said 'change X to Y' and the writeback patched 'change X to Z' (Haiku error), re-audit may pass but the wrong content lands. Fix shape: pure deterministic function in runtime/orchestration-verifier.ts compares the patched spec content against the expected post-patch state derived from the PM's recommendation text (string-match the 'to' value in the spec; assert exactly the predicted change). Per Principle 11 (deterministic audits). M0-required because Principle 17 cross-surface consistency requires the writeback to do exactly what the upstream message claimed.

items.title: Architect's turn auto-chains upstream resolution → next-phase check → escalate or proceed (orchestration Gap D)
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-04-30 in BACKLOG.md lines 182-196, formalized as Block O 2026-05-01. Today each phase requires a separate user prompt — architect resumes after PM closes, then user has to nudge architect to check design next, then user has to nudge again to proceed to engineering. Fix shape: state machine in V2 architect runner that, in one continuation: confirms PM gaps closed → re-audits design → escalates to designer if needed → otherwise proceeds to engineering finalize. End-to-end integration scenario in tests/integration/workflows.test.ts asserts the full chain runs with zero user nudges. M0-required.

items.title: Designer-side symmetry of O1 + O2 (orchestration Gap E)
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-04-30 in BACKLOG.md lines 182-196, formalized as Block O 2026-05-01. Per Principle 15 (cross-agent parity), the O1 closure clause and O2 auto-trigger architect resume must also exist on the designer-originated escalation paths (architect→designer Gap A symmetric, architect→designer Gap B symmetric). Fix shape: extend O1's shared closure-clause module to designer-originated escalations; extend O2's auto-trigger to fire when designer's escalation resolution writes back to design spec → architect auto-resumes. Cross-agent invariant test enforces parity. M0-required.
